name: portrm
tagline: Stop guessing what's running on your machine.
description: portrm is a fast, cross-platform CLI to inspect ports, understand processes, and recover broken dev environments - built for real-world development workflows.
short_description: portrm is a fast, cross-platform CLI to inspect ports, understand processes, and recover broken dev environments - built for real-world development workflows.

packages.homebrew: brew tap abhishekayu/tap && brew install portrm

install.homebrew: brew tap abhishekayu/tap && brew install portrm

commands.name: scan
commands.description: List all listening ports with process name, PID, service type, memory, uptime, and user.

commands.name: info
commands.description: Inspect a single port with detailed process and service information.

commands.name: fix
commands.description: Identify process on port, run safety checks, gracefully kill, suggest restart.

commands.name: group
commands.description: Group listening ports by role: frontend, backend, database, infrastructure.

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Auto-diagnose dev environment: stale servers, idle processes, port conflicts.

commands.name: history
commands.description: View past ptrm actions with timestamps and outcomes.

commands.name: project
commands.description: Detect project type and suggest dev commands.

commands.name: ui
commands.description: Interactive terminal UI with keyboard navigation for port management.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Create a .ptrm.toml configuration file in the current directory.

commands.name: up
commands.description: Start all services defined in .ptrm.toml with pre-flight port checks.

commands.name: down
commands.description: Stop all services defined in .ptrm.toml by gracefully killing processes.

commands.name: preflight
commands.description: Check if ports are free before starting. Reads from .ptrm.toml if no ports given.

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Monitor a port continuously. Detects crashes and auto-restarts from .ptrm.toml config.

commands.name: restart
commands.description: Restart a named service from .ptrm.toml. Stops the process on the port (local or Docker), then re-runs the configured command.

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show live status of all services from .ptrm.toml. Reports Running, Stopped, or Conflict for each service.

commands.name: log
commands.description: Stream live logs from a port. Supports Docker containers and local processes with log file detection.

tiers.description: System-critical processes. ptrm refuses to kill.
Databases and infrastructure. Warns about data loss risk.

comparison.vs_kill_port: ptrm adds safety checks, service classification, graceful shutdown, restart hints, doctor, TUI. Native binary vs Node.js.
comparison.vs_fkill: ptrm adds safety assessment, graceful shutdown, project-aware restart, auto-diagnosis. Native binary vs Node.js.
comparison.vs_lsof_kill: portrm is a single command that identifies, assesses safety, kills gracefully, and suggests restart. No manual PID parsing.
comparison.vs_docker_compose: ptrm up/down manages local processes without containers. Lightweight alternative for local dev stacks.
